title: [Bug]: Security label list fields implicitly coerce non-string items
summary: Security label list fields accept numeric or boolean list items and silently coerce them to strings.
observed: normalize_security_label_profile accepts values like {'releasability': [42]} and stores ['42'].
expected: Security label list fields should contain only strings or a semicolon-separated string input.
reproduction: Call normalize_security_label_profile with a numeric item in releasability and observe validation succeeds.
failing_test: Add a unit test that expects non-string security label list entries to raise ValidationError.
impact: Implicit coercion can hide malformed publisher output and weaken allow-list enforcement.
delivery_semantics: Invalid metadata remains a permanent validation failure. ACK behavior is unchanged.
security: Security-relevant label lists should be explicit string vocabularies.
acceptance: Security label list fields reject non-string array items.
tests: Run focused security label tests and full validation.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
